metadata.generated_notice: Generated file - do not edit manually.

envoy.blocking: realistic for request blocking with ext_authz/ext_proc; response-body disruptive semantics require explicit filter-mode proof
envoy.integration: realistic via ext_proc gRPC sidecar or ext_authz decision service; WASM/Lua are possible but not currently represented in the repo
envoy.limits: body coverage is governed by Envoy buffering/streaming settings and partial-body behavior; must be evidenced per case
envoy.phases: request headers/body and response headers/body are conceptually mappable through Envoy HTTP filters, subject to configured processing modes

lighttpd.blocking: realistic only after the chosen hook can stop forwarding before upstream commit
lighttpd.integration: no native ModSecurity adapter is present; needs either a native module study or proxy/sidecar bridge
lighttpd.limits: unknown until module/proxy architecture and buffering behavior are selected
lighttpd.phases: request headers/body are more likely first; response phases need separate hook proof

litespeed.blocking: plausible if the server's own ModSecurity engine can be driven in CI with CRS fixtures
litespeed.integration: OpenLiteSpeed and LiteSpeed Enterprise advertise ModSecurity-compatible rule support, but this repo has no connector or harness
litespeed.limits: license, package availability, edition differences, and non-libmodsecurity engine compatibility are the main risks
litespeed.phases: must be mapped empirically from server logs/results rather than assumed equivalent to libmodsecurity v3 connectors

traefik.blocking: request blocking is realistic through middleware/decision service; response-body blocking is higher risk
traefik.integration: plugin/middleware or forwardAuth-style decision service are the likely paths; direct libmodsecurity integration is not proven
traefik.limits: depends on plugin/middleware body buffering and Yaegi/Go runtime constraints
traefik.phases: request header/body first; response phases require Go middleware/harness evidence
